components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Carbon-limited Evans defined medium was used with 21.9 mM glucose as the carbon source

components.name: monosodium phosphate (NaH2PO4)
components.evidence: Evans medium (10 mM NaH2PO4, 10 mM KCl, 1.25 mM MgCl2, 100 mM NH4Cl, 2 mM Na2SO4, 2 mM nitrilotriacetic acid, 0.02 mM CaCl2, 25 uM ZnO, 10 uM FeCl3, 50 uM MnCl2, 5 uM CuCl2, 11 uM CoCl2, 0.05 uM H3BO3, 0.08 uM Na2MoO4)

components.evidence: A carbon-limited defined medium was used ... It was based on that of Evans et al. except that 2 mM nitrilotriacetic acid was used as the metal chelator instead of citrate

components.name: sodium selenite (Na2SeO3)
components.evidence: (10 mM NaH2PO4 ... 0.08 uM Na2MoO4) supplemented with 30 ug l-1 sodium selenite, and 20 mM glucose as a carbon source

components.name: carbon dioxide / dinitrogen gas phase (anaerobic sparge)
components.role: gas phase / anaerobic sparge (CO2 also inorganic C/bicarbonate)
components.evidence: Anaerobic cultures were maintained by sparging with N2 (95%) and CO2 (5%) at 0.4 l min-1

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.concentration: dissolved O2 held at 5% (~10 uM) in micro-aerobic phase
components.evidence: The switch to micro-aerobic conditions was achieved by automatically altering the gas mix ratio of N2 and CO2 to air to maintain the dissolved O2 tension in the culture at 5% (~10 uM)

media.base_medium: Evans defined medium, carbon(glucose)-limited variant; metal chelator = 2 mM nitrilotriacetic acid instead of citrate (Evans, Herbert & Tempest 1970, Methods Microbiol 2:277-327, ref [22])

sources_chased.what: PMC3181329 full text (Trotter et al. 2011, PLoS ONE), main-text medium/growth statements
Base recipe cited as ref [22] Evans, Herbert & Tempest 1970 (book chapter, no DOI/PMID)
sources_chased.how: primary book chapter not online; recovered via same-lab papers reproducing the exact formulation

sources_chased.what: Same-lab full Evans formulation (Green group) - Adaptation of anaerobic E. coli to TMAO, PMC4949985
Same-lab companion Royal Soc Open Sci (Homeostasis of metabolites), PMC5108944
added: confirmed NTA (2 mM) replaces citrate as chelator; carbon-limited glucose Evans medium

media.notes: PMC3181329 gives only 21.9 mM glucose + gas/pH in its own text and cites the Evans 1970 book chapter for the rest. The full salt/trace/vitamin set was recovered verbatim from two open-access papers by the SAME lab (Green group, Sheffield) using the identical carbon-limited Evans defined medium (PMC4949985 gives the complete component-by-component formulation; PMC5108944 confirms 2 mM NTA replaces citrate). Corrections vs current_components: phosphate is 10 mM NaH2PO4 (not 50 mM K2HPO4); K source is 10 mM KCl; iron is FeCl3 (ferric) at 10 uM; molybdenum is Na2MoO4 at 0.08 uM (not ammonium molybdate); MgCl2 1.25 mM, CaCl2 0.02 mM, NH4Cl 100 mM (N in excess, carbon-limited). Added ZnO, H3BO3, NTA, and Na2SeO3. No amino acids/vitamins in this defined medium; anaerobiosis achieved by N2/CO2 gas sparge with no chemical reductant. glucose set to 21.9 mM per PMC3181329 (sister papers use 20 mM).
